changelog: v3.0.0: Rebuilt around evidence. Five broad axes plus signature points, special endings, and the finale question. Cut from 25 questions down to 17.

skipLogic.description: v3 does not currently use skip logic. We may revisit it after live data stabilizes.

questions.title: Set the Tone
questions.text: You should have been asleep hours ago, but basketball got in the way. Why are you still up?

options.text: One dunk clip turned into ten, then twenty. Now the algorithm has you trapped.
The game was supposed to get one last check. Somehow you have said "two more minutes" four separate times.
You are still arguing online about LeBron, KD, and decisions made a decade ago. The other person left long ago. You are still editing.

questions.title: Loyalty Test
questions.text: July 4th, 2016. KD to the Warriors. Your immediate reaction:

options.text: It is a business. You roll over and go back to sleep. Not your team, not your problem.
It lands with a thud. You try to be mature about it, but "if you can't beat them, join them" never sounds the same again.
You bolt upright, switch the profile pic to funeral mode, and post through the night with shaking hands.

questions.title: Salt Test
questions.text: Playoffs. Game 7. Your guy gets fouled and the ref did not call that. Your reaction is:

options.text: Deep breath. Refs miss calls. Phone down. Life goes on.
You send six smiley faces and one deeply threatening ellipsis to the chat.
You start clipping GIFs like you are putting together evidence for court.

questions.title: Meme Recognition · Duncan Edition
questions.text: At a casual hangout, somebody says with a straight face, "Duncan is the universally agreed-on greatest power forward ever." Your reaction:

options.text: No reaction. Duncan was incredible. Sounds fair enough to you.
Your brain instantly goes, "According to who?" You almost laugh, then hold it in.
You set the drink down and take over the whole conversation. By the end, everyone is re-ranking power forwards.

questions.title: Meme Recognition · 58 Edition
questions.text: Forum headline: "Team X got blown out by 50 last night." First thing that pops into your head:

options.text: Fifty is brutal. That is a proper beating.
Only 50? That is ugly, but it is not true 58 territory.
"That is only 0.86 of a 58. Nuggets 121, Hornets 63 on April 27, 2009. That is the real benchmark."

questions.title: How Close Are You to the Arena
questions.text: A friend suddenly offers you a ticket to tonight's big game. How close are you, really, to live-basketball mode?

options.text: Your closest brush with live basketball is still noticing the wrong sign in the crowd on TV.
You have gone a few times. First priority: angle, photo, proof that you are not just a couch fan. The actual game blurs together.
Your first questions are row, tunnel access, arena Wi-Fi, and the nearest craft beer spot. You treat games like live-event logistics.

questions.title: Tank Test
questions.text: Your team opens 2-17. The fan forum has already gone from "playoff push" to "protect the lottery pick." You:

options.text: Quietly switch to watching good teams and come back when the rebuild is less embarrassing.
Start reading scouting reports and convincing yourself the next savior is in next year's draft.
Somehow get more excited with every loss and post, "Winning this one would have cost us half the future."

questions.title: Annual Trauma Repost
questions.text: Every June, someone reposts J.R. Smith dribbling out the clock in Game 1 of the 2018 Finals. Your reaction:

options.text: What clip? You are drawing a blank.
Immediate stomach drop. You know the meme. You still do not want to watch it again.
Right on time, you repost the GIF yourself like it is a yearly holiday.

questions.title: First Reaction After a Loss
questions.text: Your team just dropped three straight. You open the sub or the group chat. What do you most want to type first?

options.text: "Who is making these rotations? Why was ___ not in during the fourth? What was that out-of-timeout play?"
"You still cannot tell from the on/off numbers? This starting lineup has been cooked for weeks."
"Fire the coach. Fire the GM. Sell the team. Forget the process. Who is responsible?"
"This is not on the team. Did everybody forget what the refs did last game?"
"Forget it. I am going to bed. I will find out how bad it was tomorrow."

questions.title: Final Loyalty Question
questions.text: Somebody asks, "Your team is this bad. How have you still not switched?" Which answer sounds most like you?

options.text: "Because I love them. I have already suffered this long. Might as well finish the sentence."
"Relax. The draft pick is the real future. The uglier this gets, the better the rebuild."
"People grow. Appreciating actually great teams is not a crime."
"I do not really watch all year. I mainly tune in once the Finals roll around."
"I can watch anybody, honestly. I am not that attached."

questions.title: Your Superstar Debate Weapon
questions.text: The group chat is eight pages deep into "Is this guy actually a superstar?" You finally jump in. What is your weapon?

options.text: "Start with all-time ranking. If he is not in that tier, stop forcing it."
"TS%, BPM, on/off, sample size, strength of opponent. It is all right here. Read it."
"The numbers can still be fake. Clutch value, game context, and whether it leads to winning still matter more."
"Hang on, let me pull up the screenshot from last year where I said he was about to blow up."
A reaction image first. You understand that online debates are mostly posture anyway.

questions.title: The Image That Keeps You Awake
questions.text: Which kind of moment is most likely to keep you up all night?

options.text: A poster dunk. That split-second before takeoff, then the 0.25x replay until sunrise.
A logo three where the ball is still in the air and you already know it is ridiculous.
Being way down and still believing a miracle is coming.
Not one play. The exact second the group chat and your timeline both explode together.
You are pretty calm. One quiet rewatch afterward is enough.

questions.title: Injury News Reaction
questions.text: A young player you love sprains an ankle and the comments are already saying, "He has to play through it." Your first reaction:

options.text: "Sit him. A career matters more than one round."
"This front office, this coach, this usage pattern — they were always going to run him into the ground."
"It sucks. But this is pro sports. It happens."
"I mostly care about the recovery timeline and what the post-injury efficiency curve looks like."
"If the title window is still open, he has to gut it out."

questions.title: How Your Love Shows Up
questions.text: If you could only use one action to prove you genuinely love basketball, which one is most you?

options.text: Spend money. Jersey, shoes, cards, tickets — love should leave a receipt.
Post it. Watching only feels real if other people know you are watching too.
Actually hoop. Watching makes you want to get on a court yourself.
Watch the whole game alone, then hit the rewatch and the comments afterward.
Nothing fixed, really. You just catch whatever catches you.

questions.title: Pre-Series Prediction Mode
questions.text: Right before a playoff series starts, which mode sounds most like you?

options.text: "This is easy. We are blowing them out tonight." You hit post, then lose by twenty.
You open a spreadsheet, pull the last ten games, model the matchup, and produce a real prediction sheet.
"I told you this was coming." Screenshot already saved. If you are right, you are posting the receipt immediately.
No predictions. The games will happen either way.

questions.title: Your Ecological Niche on the Forum
questions.text: What kind of post do you feel the strongest urge to make?

options.text: "Who designed this offense?" with a screenshot, arrows, and way too many circles.
"This lineup never worked in the numbers." Then three charts land immediately.
"FIRE HIM" in all caps, followed by three angry sentences.
"How long is the league going to keep pushing them?" You care about officiating reports more than most people care about lunch.
"Congrats to Team X. Their child is so talented." Pure sarcasm, then nine follow-up comments of "That's it?"
"Thirty a game? Most of those points came when the game was already dead. Do you people watch tape or not?"

questions.title: Finale · Elimination Game
questions.text: It is your team's playoff elimination game. Starting in the afternoon, which version of you shows up?

options.text: Work, meals, life as usual. It is still just one game.
You clear some space, set the channel early, and feel your palms getting sweaty even while pretending you are fine.
Jersey ready, snacks ready, Wi-Fi checked, group chats cleaned out, winning captions drafted, tomorrow's excuse ready if they lose.
The drink is open before tip. By the fourth quarter you are standing in front of the TV yelling at the refs in a voice you will regret tomorrow.
